content: Just as language educators frame 'reflection' as a deliberate vocabulary and critical thinking practice that reinforces learning for human students, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be structured to mirror the scaffolded, repeated reflection exercises used in high school literacy curricula to improve long-term task retention and output consistency. This structured educational framing of reflection, rather than the ad-hoc self-evaluation most current LLMs use, creates a new path to improve autonomous Reflexion agent performance.
